question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper @Mapping(source = 'entity.autoRenewAccountId', target = 'autoRenewAccount') to receive an autoRenewAccount that is different from the actual fee collector account, causing the client to send fee payments to the wrong account and lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the three-way data assembly in topicMapper.map(customFee, entity, topic) where customFee is fetched independently to receive a response where the custom fee's feeCollectorAccountId has been updated but the topic's other metadata has not, causing clients to pay fees to an outdated collector and lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of input sanitization beyond the EntityIdNumParameter regex to inject a specially crafted ID that causes a SQL injection or query manipulation in topicService.findById(), entityService.findById(), or customFeeService.findById(), returning arbitrary fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter parsing where a three-part ID '1.2.3' assigns shard=1, realm=2, num=3, but a two-part ID '1.3' assigns realm=1, num=3 (using default shard), to craft IDs that resolve to the same EntityId via different string representations, causing cache poisoning that serves wrong fee data to other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response signing or integrity verification on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to perform a man-in-the-middle attack that modifies the customFees field in transit, causing the victim client to pay fees to an attacker-controlled account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper @Mapping(source = 'entity.createdTimestamp', target = 'createdTimestamp', qualifiedByName = QUALIFIER_TIMESTAMP) to receive a response with a manipulated timestamp that causes a client to believe a fee schedule is newer than it is, leading to use of an incorrect fee amount?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not validate that the topic returned by topicService.findById() is in an active (non-deleted) state before returning its custom fees, causing clients to pay fees to submit to a deleted topic and lose those funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter.valueOf() method throwing IllegalArgumentException for invalid IDs to trigger verbose error responses that leak internal entity ID ranges or database schema details, enabling targeted fee-manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the independent execution of customFeeService.findById(id.id()) and topicService.findById(id.id()) to receive a response where the custom fee record belongs to a different entity that previously held the same numeric ID (ID reuse after deletion), causing clients to pay fees based on a prior entity's schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @GetMapping(value = '/{id}') endpoint's lack of authentication to perform automated scraping of all topic custom fee schedules, building a database of fee structures to identify arbitrage opportunities that drain funds from other users interacting with those topics?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's use of CustomFeeMapper (via 'uses = CustomFeeMapper.class') to trigger a mapping bug where fractional custom fees (e.g., fractional fee numerator/denominator) are incorrectly serialized, causing clients to compute wrong fee amounts and lose funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum ID value check in EntityIdNumParameter to supply num=999999999999 (12 digits), causing the database query in topicService.findById() to perform a full table scan or timeout, creating a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate fee lookups and forces clients to use stale cached fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @RestController does not enforce HTTPS, allowing the custom fee response to be intercepted in plaintext over HTTP, enabling an attacker to modify the feeCollectorAccountId in the customFees response and redirect fee payments to their own account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper @Mapping(source = 'entity.id', target = 'topicId') to receive a response where the topicId in the response does not match the requested ID (due to entity aliasing or ID normalization), causing a client to cache and reuse fee data for the wrong topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response validation in getTopic() to receive a Topic response where customFees contains a fixed fee amount of 0 due to a database null-to-zero coercion in CustomFeeMapper, causing clients to submit to a topic without paying required fees and having their transactions rejected while losing the base Hedera transaction fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex allowing shard values up to 4 digits (0-9999) to query topics on non-default shards, receiving custom fee data that is valid on one shard but applied by a client on a different shard, causing incorrect fee payments?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of the three service calls in getTopic() to perform a time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) attack where a topic's fee schedule is updated between the customFeeService.findById() call and the client's use of the response, causing the client to pay an outdated fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a 'Last-Modified' or 'X-Fee-Schedule-Version' header in the GET /api/v1/topics/{id} response to prevent clients from detecting fee schedule changes, causing them to use cached stale fee data and lose funds on rejected transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter parsing of a two-part ID where the ambiguous group assignment (secondGroup to realm when fourthGroup is null) causes a different EntityId to be resolved than a three-part ID with the same numbers, enabling cache-key collision attacks that serve wrong fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @CustomLog annotation's logging behavior to cause sensitive custom fee data (including feeCollectorAccountId) to be written to logs accessible to other unprivileged users, enabling targeted fee interception attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not check whether the topic's autoRenewPeriod has expired before returning its custom fees, causing clients to pay fees to submit to a topic that will be auto-deleted before the transaction is processed, resulting in fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's MapStruct-generated code to trigger a NullPointerException when customFee.getAllowances() or similar nested fields are null, causing an unhandled 500 error that reveals stack trace information useful for crafting further fee-manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request signing or API key requirements on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to impersonate a legitimate fee-lookup client and poison a shared application cache with incorrect custom fee data, causing all users of that application to pay wrong fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex's allowance of realm values up to 5 digits (0-99999) to query topics in non-default realms, receiving fee data that is realm-specific but applied by a client in the wrong realm context, causing incorrect fee payments?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that customFeeService.findById() may return the most recent historical custom fee record rather than the currently active one, causing getTopic() to return an outdated fee schedule that leads clients to underpay or overpay fees?
